title: Trischidium molle
taxonomy: Trischidium molle is classified as a species within the genus Trischidium and the family Polygalaceae. This classification places it within the order Polygalales, a group of flowering plants commonly known as the milkwort family. The family Polygalaceae is part of the larger clade Rosidae, which includes a diverse range of flowering plant families. Within the Polygalaceae family, Trischidium molle is one of several species, each with distinct characteristics and growth habits. This classification helps to identify and differentiate Trischidium molle from other related plant species.

Trischidium molle has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. However, the efficacy and safety of using Trischidium molle for medicinal purposes require further scientific investigation.

intro: Trischidium molle is a tropical plant species belonging to the family Fabaceae, commonly known as a soft-leaved Trischidium or velvet Trischidium due to its soft, velvety leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and gastrointestinal issues.
